other_observers: Dave Wade, John Shenot, Austin Hess
description: Small sparrow singing from perches atop thistle weeds in a small section of grassland, and also disappearing among grasses at ground level; very pale (white) base color to breast but otherwise viewed too distantly or too briefly to assess plumage details. Only one bird seen. Two birds gave unique Baird's Sparrow song simultaneously. There may have been more than two singing males, as at different times, songs were heard from at least three different directions.
